title: Keep query-side anti-collapse branch and move to bounded predicted-side repair
summary: The current active idea branch stays selected after a fresh literature and code-path sweep. The relation repair is now treated as a completed negative result, and the next bounded route should be a predicted-side anti-collapse repair on the same frozen widened packet surface, with variance-floor regularization preferred over another teacher-side repair or immediate interface redesign.
reason: Two measured teacher-side repair families already failed, the target branch is already fixed and asymmetric, and the current code path exposes predicted packet tensors that are a direct fit for explicit variance control.

alternatives.name: candidate_b_decorrelation_or_redundancy_reduction_followup
alternatives.reason_not_selected: Broader objective change and less interpretable than the smallest variance-first repair.

alternatives.name: candidate_c_return_to_interface_redesign
alternatives.reason_not_selected: Direct predicted-side collapse evidence is still too strong to skip one bounded predicted-side repair.

strongest_support: Two measured teacher-side repair families already failed, the target branch is already fixed and asymmetric, and the current code path exposes predicted packet tensors that are a direct fit for explicit variance control.
main_residual_risk: The true bottleneck may lie earlier than the currently accessible packet tensors, so even a clean predicted-side repair may only produce a clearer negative result.
implementation_note: artifact.submit_idea(mode=revise) still fails in the current runtime with an unsupported active-anchor error, so the refreshed route was preserved through branch files plus this decision artifact.
comment: Record the refreshed idea-route judgment durably after submit_idea revise hit the known anchor bug

guidance_vm.summary: Decision accepted: launch the main experiment on the selected route.
guidance_vm.why_now: Two measured teacher-side repair families already failed, the target branch is already fixed and asymmetric, and the current code path exposes predicted packet tensors that are a direct fit for explicit variance control.
complete_when: The main run is executed and stored as a run artifact.
Key metrics versus baseline are written durably.

alternative_routes.label: Use the active branch
alternative_routes.when: The accepted idea already owns an isolated branch/worktree.
alternative_routes.tradeoff: Keeps the normal path simple, but assumes the idea branch is in good shape.

alternative_routes.label: Analyze first
alternative_routes.when: The setup still has unresolved confounders.
alternative_routes.tradeoff: Reduces implementation waste, but delays primary evidence.

suggested_artifact_calls.name: artifact.record_main_experiment(...)
suggested_artifact_calls.purpose: Persist the main experiment outcome on the active idea branch.
